review_title: Account Executive
author: Jeffrey N.
pros: Love how this tool integrates with analytics, and is able to compare our keywords against a short list of our competitors.
cons: It was a bit clunky to use when we first started working with it, but with the help from the Brightedge team, we were able to navigate through it. After a year of use, we are extremely happy with the results.

review_title: Overpriced compared to its competitors
description: I think BrightEdge is best suited for large companies with large SEO teams and big budgets.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The main pro of BrightEdge over something like SEMrush is the ability to assign keywords to specific landing pages, and then track ranking of those specific pages. Additionally, they assign you a "success manager" who meets with you regularly to get the most out of the tool. Our success manager was great.
cons: It's too expensive. At least 3x what we are paying for an alternative solution that we like better. The account managers do not honor your decisions, and will try to go above you if you want to leave. The [SENSITIVE CONTENT] even sent an email to my boss's boss, trying to argue that the marketing team is making a poor decision. The tool itself can be overwhelming and requires too much work to make the high cost worthwhile. The fact that they need to assign you a success partner to make sure you are using it, says a lot.
author_profession: Director of IT and Digital Marketing

review_title: A lot of improvement is needed
author: Chiara G.
pros: Honestly I struggle to find something I like, maybe the dashboards? It's easy to implement, very easy
cons: I don't like this tool at all, it's not intuitive at all, the ui is not user-friendly, the tool has a lot of functionality but they're mainly scattered all over. US is always the main country which makes new functionalities not available for the rest of the world

review_title: Excellent tool for SEO. Has all the functionality one could need.
author: Stephanie E.
pros: Recommendations in the system based on keywords and preferred pages by keyword. Great site health audit check which lays out what needs to be fixed, by importance. (You can even assign value to to pages in Google Analytics, so you get a better idea of what to fix first.) Amazing team behind the product who is always looking for customer input to improve and add options. Assign specific tasks to people by function, such as assign a developer to fix a speed issue or assign a designer to add a meta description to a page. Track the progress of tasks assigned. System will auto mark something done when it detects a task is completed. Just an amazing product in so many ways. Training and certification cannot be beaten. My favorite part of Brightedge is that in the keyword analysis you can find high value, low competition keywords to write about. This helped us attain first page presence for some very high value keywords. We also used their help to start creating pages with Google Schema and had amazing results with that.
cons: Their onboarding is excellent, yet it still takes some time to get used to the entire system of adding researching and adding keywords, mapping a keyword to a preferred page, and then getting and completing tasks. Once you understand it and get used to how it works, it is awesome. However you have to be extremely committed and spend quite a bit of time on a regular basis to make that happen.

review_title: Average Enterprise SEO Tool
description: I thought the experience with Brightedge was okay. Not great, not horrible, but not worth the money that was being allocated in the contract.
author: Ian K.
pros: There are two favorite features of Brightedge. The first is Brightedge content, which allows you to do keyword research and then write an optimized post from inside Brightedge. Not only will this content then live on your website, but you can also add custom calls to action for lead gen. This is a really helpful solution if you are using an enterprise CMS that isn't user friendly for regularly publishing content. The other feature that I really like is the in-depth reporting on keyword performance and the ability to see all of your SEO metrics in one view.
cons: The actual product is average. The cons here definitely lay in the costs and customer support. For what you are charged per year, it is quite a hefty amount, and I'm confident that I could have achieved the same ROI on organic traffic from a cheaper tool. The second con is customer support. They have some wonderful account managers, and then there are some that miss the mark. The sub-par account managers have a hard time grasping bigger picture marketing and project management. While this isn't everyone, it is a gamble.

review_title: A waste of money and a waste of time.
description: Not good. Its insanely expensive, we have voiced serious concern without any change in their behavior and the product has been a massive let down.
author: Joseph J.
pros: Not much honestly. It did provide good insight into a few pages returning 404 errors through.
cons: The implementation team did not seem to really know what was going on. The sales process made the implementation seem like a breeze. We were also lead to believe that the tool was a lot more intelligent than it turns out. It has taken us over 3 months just to get things set up and have seen no value. The support ends up being workarounds instead of legitimate solutions. Also, the service just doesn't work well, almost every time I use Brightedge it crashes, the UX is horrible and clumsy, and it requires so much manual entry.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Holistic SEO Analytics Tool
description: My overall experience with BrightEgde tool has been great when it comes to usability of its interface and the search analytics it provides once the integration with Adobe Analytics tool is successfully implemented. I was able to get quick insights into factors or search signals which are impacting our e-commerce website's organic rankings on Google.com and Bing.com. Based on those insights, I was able to suggest content and internal linking changes to improve product relevant traffic and conversions which lifted the ROI of site.
author: Gaurav G.
pros: Using BrightEdge tool, I was able to analyze organic search campaigns holistically. I was able to compare organic search rankings of the landing pages my copy team optimized the content of to themselves over a period of time as well as to competitor sites' landing pages. Additionally, I was using the tool to understand which keywords have high search volume and potential to convert to drive higher sales. By optimizing landing page organic content and keywords for search engines, I was able to improve roi of e-commerce.
cons: BrightEdge tool depends on integration with analytics tools such as Adobe or Google to maximize its potential. Unless appropriate connections between those tools are made, BrightEdge won't be able to show the impact of higher organic rankings for specific search keywords on a website's traffic to landing pages and the revenue being generated from it. Additionally, api from Google search console and Adwords are needed to compare how organic search results are trending versus paid search campaigns performance.
author_profession: E-commerce Web Analytics & SEO Specialist III

review_title: Robust Offerings But Complicated
description: I use BrightEdge because one of my SEO clients uses the tool, and while we report and track keywords in BrightEdge because we are asked to by the client , I much prefer SEMrush for everyday research. I will say that BrightEdge support is top notch.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: BrightEdge's trending tool is more effective than Google Trends research—it has some great month-over-month data and the visualization of those changes is easier to view and assess.
cons: An essential component of any SEO tool is keyword research, and BrightEdge is quite complicated. Once you understand the tool, it's very effective and more powerful than other tools in many ways. But for the ordinary user, SEMrush is far easier to understand. Everything in BrightEdge seems to take an extra step compared to Moz or SEMrush or Ahrefs. Again, you can also dig very deep, but that is beyond what many need from an SEO tool.

review_title: Definitely not worth enterprise spend!
author: Cole R.
pros: The tool has some decent integrationsa and the cuce is pretty nice. I also really like the visualizations.
cons: I really liked the quick answer reporting that the tool did when that feature first came out. I found though that it was massively inaccurate. I also found that to be the case with a lot of their ranking reports. They don't even offer robust local ranking reports(true local maps ranking).

review_title: Expensive, overly complicated and locked into a long contract
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: good SEO tools, lots of help from their customer success staff
cons: super complicated and convoluted. you can find the information you're looking for elsewhere more easily.

review_title: Scam -- don't do it
description: Did not provide what was promised, despite an enormous annual subscription fee (esp relative to competitors like Ahrefs). Tried to enforce auto-renewal and hid behind legal team, despite good faith notice. They are not ethical people.
author: Charles H.
pros: Easy to implement. It didn't like us a long time to get it live.
cons: Did not provide what was promised, despite an enormous annual subscription fee (esp relative to competitors like Ahrefs). Tried to enforce auto-renewal and hid behind legal team, despite good faith notice. They are not ethical people.

review_title: An SEO powerhouse that blends sales and effort
description: Once you start using this tool it will follow you to every company you go to. It really tells us where we can get the most bang for our buck when it comes to making changes.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The absolute best part about BrightEdge is its ability to predict how much value each SEO change is worth.
cons: BrightEdge still could be improved thought more advanced data exports. We use Google Data Studio and Domo and native connections with full data access would be wonderful.

review_title: Great tool for SEO
author: Luis L.
pros: It's important to review SEO actions, progress and main KPI's. Through this tool you can keep up with your website performance, especially on the SEO side.
cons: You need to know at least a little about SEO to use it.

review_title: The most efficient tool for SEO
description: BrightEdge is a fantastic tool which has really helped us in increasing our presence on organic search. Organic search is a very key component of our digital marketing strategy and this tool makes it a highly efficient process to drive leads & sales.
author: Mayank K.
pros: Real Estate is a localized business and local SEO is very very important as keywords, competitors and Search results ranks vary for different locations even within the city. The tool helps in showing ranks and recommendations for individual keywords which is fantastic for a business like ours.
cons: There is very little to dislike but the cost of the tool is fairly high. However, considering the detail which the tool is able to capture the cost does seem justified.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: great set of tools for SEO and organization
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Great set of tools and features. Great when working with multiple domains. Learning tools provided have been a great asset.
cons: No true cons that I can think of. Maybe choose the correct tool in the beginning?
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great software; High price
description: The customer service was top notch. The software was fantastic, but the price point was extremely high - especially when we are able to get many of the some features at a much lower price.
author: Jill J.
pros: I really loved the ease of use, the reporting functionality, the website optimization recommendations, etc.
cons: For our industry there was little competitive research, keyword suggestions, etc.

review_title: Almost as clunky and poorly built as it is expensive.
author: Ivy P.
pros: - It has a robust set of features - if they all worked correctly- Autopilot is in theory, a fantastic feature. - Opportunity forecasting would have been great, but due to the number of bugs it has, it was unusable- Data Cube, site auditing tools, content marketing tools are good, but not better than what SEMrush, Ahrefs, Moz, etc. have at a fraction of the cost
cons: - At over $55,000/year, the only key features it has over SEMRush, Moz, and Ahrefs ended up not working at all. We were locked into a full year and still use SEMRush and Ahrefs. Waste of money!- How clunky and slow everything is. Power users hate the UI and it takes twice as long to use any of their core features- Lack of QA. - Very faulty keyword research tools with extremely inaccurate, buggy results- Autopilot integration was pretty bad. It ended up breaking our build and their internal teams had no clue how to fix it

review_title: Loved This Tool and My Clients Couldn't Live Without It
author: Dana G.
pros: Software quality was superb. Data visualization was second to none, easy to "tell a story" with the data output from this product.
cons: Sometimes had data outages where previous month's ranking data was not available until the 8th or 10th of the following month. Also load times for certain data sets could be slow or delayed.

review_title: All in one SEO Tool from BrightEdge
description: I used BrightEdge for a long time drive my marketing strategy, understand my performance metrics and SEO management. There are social media signals that you can use or drive your strategy.
author: Mert D.
pros: BrightEdge gives important data and marketing analysis to understand your performance metrics. You can define KPI's and try to understand their performance with BrightEdge. Keyword tracking helps you analyze your performance and it's outputs.
cons: BrightEdge has wonderful dashboard to understand your performance metrics. They should add more dashboard or pre-dashboards to understand your performance metrics. It might be helpful to report your customer with those dashboards.

review_title: Super great keyword reporting
author: Evangeline S.
pros: The ability to track keywords and monitor their performance and gain insight into trending keyword ideas
cons: The dashboard can be cluttered and the data reported in data cube can be different than the keyword reporting tool which can be confusing

review_title: Highly recommend: Keyword Research and Tracking
description: Great! The Team Leads are so helpful to educate our SEO team and build useful reports.
author: Tiana G.
pros: We have been using Brightedge for a number of clients for keyword research and tracking. It is easy to use and customize to each client's business goals. Using the dashboards, keyword groups and page groups give clients an easy view to their performance without digging into the weeds.
cons: I think the backlink analysis tool could do a better job tracking new backlinks. Right now you have to ask Support to trigger a backfill.

review_title: Every SEO should use BrightEdge for enterprise tracking
author: Nick S.
pros: BrightEdge is an amazing enterprise solution with the best user support I've ever encountered. I love the platform and as well and the continuous feature improvements. I started using this primarily as a rank tracker but it does so much more. This tool ensures I'm always working on something meaningful.
cons: Some of the data could be streamlined and made easier to find. A few of the tools in the suite have a high learning curve, but once you figure them out they can be super powerful.

review_title: Has some interesting features, but difficult to utilise and cross analyse
description: Some great features and ideas, but could do with further refining on execution.
author: Adam C.
pros: Really liked the URL tracking and analysis tools. Being able to put in any URL and see how it was doing organically, what keywords were ranking against it and how it was rated from a content & SEO perspective.
cons: Keyword tracking was limited and the SOV/Competitor metrics were less useful from a comparative perspective.

review_title: Very Robust - Great Tool
author: Jared T.
pros: We've been using Brightedge to drive our SEO efforts for the past several years. The toolset is full of features and drives the workflow for our SEO team. I love the dashboard they access on a daily basis that directs their work to the highest priority efforts. We recently added their 'content' tools to the mix as well. Not as well versed here as we are just now training on the content tools- but I've been impressed with what I've seen so far.
cons: There is so much to their offering that navigating and understand how to get what you want out of the tool can be difficult at times.

review_title: Enterprise Level SEO & Content Marketing Tool
author: Jay B.
pros: Great SEO tool Datacube ContentIQ Technology Innovation Customer Support
cons: Price Set up takes time Need time to train Outdated tutorials A bit complex interface
